heading: Voluntary retirement after twenty years’ service
label: 2152 Voluntary retirement after twenty years’ service
text: Any regular commissioned officer who has completed twenty years’ active service in the Coast Guard, Navy, Army, Air Force, Marine Corps, or Space Force, or the Reserve components thereof, including active duty for training, at least ten years of which shall have been active commissioned service, may, upon his own application, in the discretion of the President, be retired from active service.
notes: Amendments2021— substituted “Marine Corps, or Space Force” for “or Marine Corps”.Pub. L. 116–2832018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–282section 291 of this title1986— struck out “, with retired pay of the grade with which retired” after “from active service”.Pub. L. 99–348
